title: Novavax Cuts 2024 Revenue Forecast on Lower COVID Vaccine Sales
text: Novavax has struggled to sell its protein-based COVID vaccines and, in turn, failed to make a significant dent in the market share of key players like Pfizer/BioNTech and Moderna. Factors outside of the company's control, including a smaller-than-expected COVID market size and an overwhelming number of vaccinations that took place in retail pharmacies, led Novavax to perform below expectations. The vaccine maker reduced its product sales forecast to $275 million to $375 million for the year, versus its earlier forecast of between $400 million and $600 million. This guidance cut reflected the continued challenges Novavax faces in competing with mRNA vaccine makers who have more established distribution networks and brand recognition. The company's inability to capture significant market share highlighted structural challenges in the COVID vaccine market, where mRNA platforms dominated after the initial vaccine rollout. Management's comment that factors were outside of their control suggested the company was facing market headwinds beyond its direct control, including the maturation of the global COVID vaccine market.
